# Feature: Help empty-state explains gap + shows content when published

<!-- auto-generated by /implement on 2026-07-17 -->

## Intent
End-user Help is useful when approved content exists; when empty, Dev/User sees WHY (0 flows / 0 approved / bundle missing) — German copy. Generic — not browo-specific.

## Preconditions
- React widget + core help resolver available
- Fixture facts/flows for published happy path

## Happy Path
- [ ] Empty help lists actionable German reasons (scan / review / publish / sync) when no published help for route
- [ ] When approved+published facts/flows exist for context, Help shows them
- [ ] development mode may show more; published mode stays gated
- [ ] Tests cover empty-state messaging and happy path
- [ ] No browo hardcodes

## Edge Cases
- [ ] Completely empty bundle → sync/scan reasons
- [ ] Flows exist globally but not for route → route-specific reason
- [ ] Pending reviews only in development copy

## Regression
- [ ] Existing widget tests (route title, published filter, loading) still pass
- [ ] Vue/Angular widgets share same DE reason strings from core

## Assumptions
- Core owns German reason strings; adapters only render

## Implementation Notes
- `packages/core/src/help/empty-state.ts` — `explainHelpGap()` with DE actionable reasons
- React/Vue/Angular widgets render reason list in empty state
- Tests: core help.test + react widget empty + published happy path
